content.text: I'll start by inspecting the cluster state to understand what's available (storage classes, existing secrets, node resources) before writing manifests.

input.command: kubectl get ns elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1 | head -30; echo "---secrets---"; kubectl -n elasticsearch get secrets 2>&1; echo "---sc---"; kubectl get storageclass 2>&1; echo "---nodes---"; kubectl get nodes -o wide 2>&1

input.command:
set -e
WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$WORKDIR"
# Self-signed CA for transport layer (node-to-node), independent of the provided HTTP CA since we don't have its key
openssl genrsa -out transport-ca.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key transport-ca.key -sha256 -days 3650 \
  -subj "/CN=es-transport-ca" -out transport-ca.crt 2>/dev/null

# Single shared node cert/key used by all 3 nodes (verification_mode=certificate, so hostname SANs aren't checked)
openssl genrsa -out transport-node.key 2048 2>/dev/null
cat > transport-node.cnf <<'EOF'
[req]
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions = ext
prompt = no
[dn]
CN = es-transport
[ext]
subjectAltName = DNS:*.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local,DNS:es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local,DNS:localhost
EOF
openssl req -new -key transport-node.key -out transport-node.csr -config transport-node.cnf 2>/dev/null
openssl x509 -req -in transport-node.csr -CA transport-ca.crt -CAkey transport-ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out transport-node.crt -days 3650 -extfile transport-node.cnf -extensions ext -sha256 2>/dev/null

ls -la
echo "WORKDIR=$WORKDIR"

-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  221 Jul 10 00:37 transport-node.cnf

content.text: Now creating the Secret holding the transport-layer certs in the cluster.

input.command:
cd "$(ls -d /tmp/tmp.* | tail -1)" 2>/dev/null || true
WORKDIR=$(ls -d /tmp/tmp.* | tail -1)
cd "$WORKDIR"
kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic es-transport-certs \
  --from-file=ca.crt=transport-ca.crt \
  --from-file=tls.crt=transport-node.crt \
  --from-file=tls.key=transport-node.key
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-transport-certs

content.content:
secret/es-transport-certs created
NAME                 TYPE     DATA   AGE
es-transport-certs   Opaque   3      0s
Shell cwd was reset to /workspace

tool_use_result.stdout:

content.text: Now let's write the ConfigMap, Services, PDB, and StatefulSet manifests.

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-0
      - es-1
      - es-2

    node.roles: [master, data, ingest]

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/ca.crt

    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/transport-certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/transport-certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/transport-certs/ca.crt

    bootstrap.memory_lock: false

content.content: File created successfully at: /workspace/es-config.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch

input.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          securityContext:
            runAsUser: 0
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: increase-vm-max-map-count
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
            runAsUser: 0
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "2Gi"
            limits:
              cpu: "2"
              memory: "2Gi"
          env:
            - name: node.name
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 9200
            - name: transport
              containerPort: 9300
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: es-config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: http-certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: transport-certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/transport-certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - bash
                - -c
                - >
                  curl -s -k -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}
                  https://127.0.0.1:9200/_cluster/health?local=true
                  -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' | grep -qE '200|503'
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 10
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: http-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
        - name: transport-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-transport-certs
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        storageClassName: standard
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

content.content: File created successfully at: /workspace/es-statefulset.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: I should use a minimal image for the init containers (sysctl/chown) rather than the Elasticsearch image, since it may lack those tools. Let me fix that.

                - bash
                - -c
                - >
                  curl -s -k -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}
                  https://127.0.0.1:9200/_cluster/health?local=true
                  -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' | grep -qE '200|503'
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 10
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: http-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
        - name: transport-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-transport-certs
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        storageClassName: standard
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
